project.name: CapSched-Linux
project.purpose: Introduce a capability-oriented execution and resource authority model into Linux scheduler foundations, eventually targeting monitor-backed domain isolation suitable for datacenter OS use.

linux_source.slice0a_status: applied; build validation pending because host flex dependency is missing

active_analysis.current_focus: complete Slice 0A build validation after installing host build dependencies; Linux Slice 0A is committed as 0b685979f27b3d42ee620ced5f707ee391a2a27f

architecture_intent.prototype_track: Linux-only CapSched L0 for performance, integration, and semantic exploration. It must not claim hypervisor-grade isolation.
architecture_intent.production_track: Monitor-backed CapSched-H: Linux-compatible domain-aware kernel plus small HyperTag Monitor enforcing non-forgeable DomainTag, epochs, MemoryView, IOMMU/queue ownership, and root CPU budgets.
architecture_intent.datacenter_goal: Provide process-to-container-granularity domains with isolation strength comparable to VM boundaries, while preserving lower cost and Linux ABI compatibility.
architecture_intent.cluster_goal: Support a cluster-wide capability/resource lease namespace without requiring one monolithic distributed kernel.

core_concepts.Domain: Schedulable protection, ownership, audit, and resource context. May represent a process, service, container, tenant, or cluster cell.
core_concepts.DomainTag: Active protection context selected at context switch; binds domain identity, epoch, capability root, MemoryView, object/device views, and side-channel policy.
core_concepts.RunCap: Authority to submit a specific thread/task for runnable execution. It must not include kill, suspend, priority, budget, affinity, spawn, or mint authority.
core_concepts.SchedContext: Independent CPU-time resource object containing budget, period, remaining runtime, allowed CPUs/NUMA, priority class, and co-tenancy constraints.
core_concepts.FrozenRunUse: Execution lease frozen at enqueue after RunCap and SchedContext checks. Runqueues must not store raw capability handles as authority.
core_concepts.EndpointCap: Resource-specific operation authority checked by resource endpoints such as VFS, socket, broker, device queue, or remote endpoint.
core_concepts.BudgetTicket: Bounded donated budget for broker/service execution, preventing service confused deputy and DoS amplification.
core_concepts.HyperTagMonitor: Small lower layer that enforces non-forgeable roots below Linux: domain registry, epochs, sealed run tokens, MemoryViews, IOMMU mappings, queue ownership, and root budgets.

accepted_invariants.text: No valid epoch and generation, no execution.
No DomainTag activation, no context switch across domains.
No async work without caller provenance and frozen authority.
No caller budget, no broker/service execution on behalf of that caller.
No raw pointer or mutable kernel object authority across Domain boundaries.
Linux-only prototypes must not claim hypervisor-grade isolation.
BPF and sched_ext may suggest or prototype policy, but must not be the production root for CapSched security invariants.
CapSched CPU placement must refine existing affinity, cpuset, sched-domain, housekeeping, and hotplug constraints rather than bypass them.

design_boundaries: Do not decide exact Linux patch points before reading current upstream code.
Do not make RunCap a universal capability.
Do not place VFS, BPF, GPU, or protocol-specific semantics inside the scheduler.
Do not use sched_ext as the production security boundary; it is useful for policy exploration.
Do not claim hypervisor replacement without monitor-enforced memory, CPU, IOMMU, queue, epoch, and domain roots.
Do not treat validation as pass/fail plumbing; validation must support semantic security claims.

open_questions.text: Which upstream Linux version and exact commit should be used as the base?
open_questions.answer: Use git.kernel.org torvalds/linux at commit 4edcdefd4083ae04b1a5656f4be6cd83ae919ef4 on work branch capsched-linux-l0.

open_questions.text: What is the smallest L0 slice that can demonstrate RunCap/SchedContext/FrozenRunUse without destabilizing scheduler fast paths?
open_questions.answer: Must now be derived from the checked Runnable Lease model and upstream scheduler path maps. No patch slice accepted yet.

open_questions.text: Which formal model should be written first: enqueue/pick/tick budget semantics, epoch revocation, or async provenance?
open_questions.answer: Start with the Runnable Lease Model: Task, TaskGeneration, ProcessGeneration, Domain, DomainEpoch, RunCap, SchedContext, FrozenRunUse, RunqueueState, CPU, and Budget. Keep async and monitor placeholders explicit.

open_questions.text: How should cluster-wide signed resource leases compile into local node SchedContexts and endpoint capabilities?

next_actions.text: Initialize Git in capsched/ for project-control artifacts, with linux/ left unignored as a sibling Linux repository.
Fetch current upstream Linux into sibling directory linux/ and record exact commit metadata.
Read scheduler, fork/exec/exit, cgroup CPU, core scheduling, sched_ext, workqueue, and io_uring code before choosing patch points.
Create investigation notes that map actual upstream code paths to CapSched invariants.
Select and scope the first formal semantic model before implementing prototype code.
Write and check the Runnable Lease formal model before selecting Linux L0 patch points.
Model EndpointCap and async provenance for socket and io_uring after the Runnable Lease model.
Derive the Linux L0 implementation plan from the checked Runnable Lease model and source-analysis maps before touching Linux code.
Review the candidate Linux L0 Runnable Lease implementation plan and explicitly choose the first no-behavior-change Linux patch slice.
Run the baseline out-of-tree Linux build and then apply/validate Slice 0A inert CONFIG_CAPSCHED scaffolding.
next_actions.partial_progress: Slice 0A Linux commit 0b685979f27b3d42ee620ced5f707ee391a2a27f is applied. Baseline and post-patch config builds are blocked by missing host command flex; sudo requires a password.
